tech

Dehumanizing Humans, Personifying Computers: Why Modern Fascism Wants You to be Lonely

Welcome to fully-automated luxury fascism. Can I take your personhood?

Andrea Grimes

10 Feb 2026 • 9 min read
Say it with us: abolish ice, free palestine, defund cops, trans rights

Contributing to our community is very important to us at The Flytrap.

All profits this month from this fantastic bandana designed by our Art Director, rommy torrico, will be going to Comunidades Organizando El Poder Y La Acción Latina, a grassroots, immigrant-led organization supporting Latine families in Minnesota.

Get 'em While They're Hot!

I keep thinking about the ICE agent who was caught on tape last month wondering aloud to a horde of his fellow goons: “It’s just like ‘Call of Duty.’” He says this almost cheerily, while wielding a weapon of war in the streets of Minneapolis. This odious and cowardly fucko, gunned up and masked, terrorizing the people of Minnesota—though, I guess in his view, Minnesotans aren’t people at all. Just a bunch of random NPCs. Fodder for the fascist high score. 

I also keep thinking about the Nvidia shill—sorry, ahemm, the company’s ”global head of generative AI for automotive”—inviting attendees of the CES 2026 tech conference to “think of the car as having a soul and being an extension of your family.” Souls for cars with computers! Am I losing my mind? Do I even have one? Am I committing automotive abuse if I don’t tuck mine in each night and read it a bedtime story?

And I keep thinking of the growing anti-abortion efforts to grant constitutional rights to zygotes, embryos, and fetuses. Such proposals pave the way for the prosecution of people who have abortions—an explicit policy goal of the anti-abortion “abolitionist” faction and one that’s becoming increasingly palatable among mainline “pro-life” leaders. The anti-abortion lobby calls this “equal protection” or “fetal personhood,” and it demands that pregnant people relinquish their own human right to reproductive self-determination.        

I don’t know how people use ChatGPT to write grocery lists. I don’t want a grocery list made by a computer designed to tell kids to kill themselves.

Among these three things, imagining soulful sentience for high-tech cars is maybe the least horrifying, though perhaps also the most dipshit. If corporations are people, computers can’t be far behind; the push for AI “personhood” is already on. But the thread of dehumanization strings them all together—the deadly secret police, the dipshit cars with feelings, and the decimation of bodily autonomy.

There are many more examples, of course. I’m sure you’re thinking of them now and when you’re doomscrolling at night and in the morning and at lunchtime and after dinner and when you wake up at 3am and don’t know what to do with yourself. You’re a human, blessed and cursed with the capacity for empathy and compassion and fear and rage and pain and joy and critical thinking and creativity. You are not a corporation or a computer or a zygote. (And if you’re an ICE agent, truly what the fuck are you doing here. That’s not a question.)     

In Fascist USA, everything except humans is human. On purpose, and by design.

Dehumanization has always been a project of fascism, of empire, of colonialism, of capitalism, of white supremacy. It is, with deplorable irony, very human. Dehumanization is one of the primary means by which people have committed, defended, excused, facilitated, perpetuated, and ignored genocide and apartheid and slavery and all manner of atrocities. You know how it works. It’s the creation of a “them” that is not “us.”

It is hardly new, but it is very now. The devaluing of disabled people as not fully human has been used to justify forced sterilization, a practice that continues today. Dehumanization is a hallmark of Trumpian-MAGA rhetoric, anti-Palestinian rhetoric, antisemitic rhetoric, racist tropes of anti-Blackness, and anti-Indigenous hatred. It is a basic premise of transphobia and the attempted erasure of trans and non-binary people. It is what the Nazi-natalists have in mind when they say (white) women should give birth or die trying.

Do you love this week's art from Art Director rommy torrico? We've got a whole collection at our merch store. We don't want to be pushy, but we DO think a "Connection is Resistance" poster would look great on your wall.

If you appreciate The Flytrap's coverage of tech culture, you might enjoy our recent roundtable on the fractured reality we're all trapped in—and the accompanying art!

Take Me to the Merch Store!

While I have been thinking of these horrible things, though, I also glimpsed a fleeting moment of true joy: I watched the delivery driver who’d just dropped a package off at my front door absolutely gallop across the street to join a couple of neighborhood kids in a snowball fight. It was such a bright flash of delight, amid all the horrors, to see a grown-ass person running toward a couple of giggly, goofy kids to join in what has to be one of humanity’s oldest games. I have no idea what was going through the delivery person’s mind when they threw their clipboard aside and crouched behind a bush—ever so stealthy in their neon safety vest—and began packing snowballs. But I’ve been thinking about the concept of running to other people ever since. 

It’s incredibly powerful to seek out other people when the fascists are shoving us versus them down our throats. Like this new wave of anti-trans and anti-abortion vigilante legislation; these laws don’t only empower people to harass and surveil their neighbors, but also provide a financial incentive for doing so.

But the fascist dehumanization project has gone even further now, because it’s no longer enough to dehumanize “them.” Now, we’re expected to accept— even to celebrate and participate in—the manufacturing of “personhood” for patently non-human entities. That’s incredibly fucked up, and it’s making a bad and bleak situation even worse.  

I don’t know how people use ChatGPT to write grocery lists. I don’t want a grocery list made by a computer designed to tell kids to kill themselves. I can just remember to buy cherry tomatoes and yogurt. If I forget, I will go back to the store. That’s fine. I don’t actually think it’s cool and good to be so unconcerned with human connection and so uninterested in cultivating the bare minimum of knowledge about myself and the world and the people around me that I can’t be bothered to write my own emails. Or my own wedding vows.

Are we so miserably lonely and isolated that we are falling in love with AI bots? Or are we miserably lonely and isolated because the fashy broligarchs need us that way—indeed, like us that way—if it means we’re using their shitty tech and propping up their bullshit bubble? I am not a mental health professional, but I doubt the solution to the loneliness crisis is to sell people on the idea that their cars and computers have feelings, or that their lives are less important than an egg’s, or that they should lock their doors, stay home, and watch complacently through the blinds while their neighbors get kidnapped unless they want to get themselves shot by the secret police.

They want us in thrall to the algorithm, scrolling away in driverless taxis lest we get a taco truck recommendation from a cab driver. They are so very threatened by even the smallest prospect of human connection.

The system built to propagate “AI”—and, importantly, the people who created and stand to benefit most from it, the Vances and the Thiels and the Musks and their whole foul cohort—is intrinsically fascist, authoritarian, and anti-democratic. It is intertwined, politically and essentially, with other right-wing priorities, such as banning abortion, erasing and oppressing trans people, tear-gassing preschoolers, and packing immigrants into concentration camps. The new personhood—personhood for anyone but people—is part and parcel, a program for fascists, capitalists, and doomers. They want us in thrall to the algorithm, scrolling away in driverless taxis lest we get a taco truck recommendation from a cab driver. They are so very threatened by even the smallest prospect of human connection.

We know this because the importance of certain kinds of labor—the kinds of labor that AI is supposed to liberate us from (but more accurately just steals, exploits, or outsources)—is increasingly devalued in this new fascist hellscape precisely because it requires human connection, expertise, and creativity.

I am talking about, for example, “PROFESSIONAL-grade logistics.” If you missed PROFESSIONAL-grade logistics, it’s something of a nascent mini-meme on the left. It’s the result of a MAGA influencer dudebro melting down on Twitter about Minnesotans providing handwarmers and other supplies to neighbors defending their communities. This pathetic chucklefuck simply couldn’t imagine community supply drops being the result of anything other than a Soros-backed woke antifa conspiracy. (And yes, of course you can read about the aforementioned pathetic chucklefuck on an AI blog.) I am also talking about the Minneapolis mom who is organizing and delivering breastmilk donations—her own, and others'—to feed babies whose mothers and parents have been disappeared by ICE, or whose families are isolating out of fear of Trump's American Gestapo. AI could fucking never; doing something this meaningful and this intimate requires human connection.

Also, AI is not going to be out on the streets, making sure folks waiting in long lines to vote stay hydrated, AI will be used for anything but feeding unhoused folks—an ostensible "crime" committed on the daily by people who give a real big fuck about the wellbeing of their neighbors and their communities. AI is in-fucking-capable of reading books to children at all, let alone in the glitter-filled drag that delights the kiddos and makes going to the library (extra) fun. AI can't teach college students about gender and history; no surprise, then, that the fascist fuckos who shit their drawers over the barest prospect of young adults learning about (scare quotes incoming) "gender ideology" have gotten cowardly educational institutions to comply in advance with their anti-trans and anti-DEI fear campaigns. AI is a plagiarism machine that exists to do the bare minimum for people who are convinced they ought to be doing even less than that. It provides no new information—indeed, it has no idea what to do with new information—because it is constitutionally, essentially, and fundamentally by design unable to adapt in real time to real people and our needs.

Don't miss the latest Cancel Me, Daddy, a Flytrap Media production...

No wonder the fascist dillweeds want us to be so reliant on new tech that gathers our data, tracks us relentlessly, and reports us to the fucking cops.

We don't have to do the work for them. They're already doing it themselves, by stealing our data and using fascist tech to creep on unsuspecting folks. To wit: Have you heard of Olive, the anti-abortion AI chatbot? If you’re an “abortion-minded woman” who finds herself on the website of one of those creepy anti-abortion fake clinics, Olive may reach out to you with disinformation, junk science, and guilt-fueled religious claptrap in an effort to talk you out of ending your pregnancy. (This one really gets me, honestly—it so clearly exposes the lie that the anti-abortion movement has been trying to sell the public for years about their deep commitment to “loving them both.” They literally do not even want to talk to the very people they believe should be forced to stay pregnant against their will! That’s how much they care! I digress.)

The purveyors of modern fascism are so scared of other people, and so full of hatred for humanity, that they’re even outsourcing the thing they like best—literally doing fascism—to computers. This is a weird but perhaps fash-ily predictable inversion of the way new technologies have been historically received by those in power. The ~ man ~ has not generally been in favor of new technologies, instead fearing such advances as threats that empower the people they seek to silence and oppress to better communicate with each other, spread information, and organize themselves against their oppressors. (See: Printing Press, The.)

But in the modern fascist landscape, the historically small-d-democratic promise of new technology has been bastardized and appropriated by powerful people and entrenched systems who have formerly disavowed such tech on the basis of that very promise—while couching its appeal as a benefit to the average person. Make your grocery lists on ChatGPT, put your #resistance organizing plans into Google Docs so Gemini can have a look; send your tiresome lefty conference-organizing emails on Outlook and make sure Copilot has a gander first and can report back to the machine-men. Make it easy on yourself, and even easier on the fascist broligarchs who have the Trump regime on speed dial.

Or: Rely on other humans, and make yourself available to them in turn. Sure, it's a risk. Rely on other people, and you will often be disappointed. You will get hurt, and you will be pissed off, and you will want to tear your hair out at some of the absolutely goofy shit your comrades get up to. But running toward each other is the only way forward, the only way some or most of us make it through this. Because all of us won’t. But even fewer of us will come out on the other side if we give in, even a little bit, to the false personhood of fascism.

This piece was edited by s.e. smith and copyedited by Nicole Froio.
